2019 FORD EDGE ST - USING PARALLEL PARK ASSIST FEATURE, I FOLLOWED PROMPTS ON SCREEN, THE CAR BACKED INTO ANOTHER VEHICLE, DAMAGING THE RIGHT REAR QUARTER-PANEL OF THE EDGE. I HAD TO TAKE OVER AND BRAKE ONCE I REALIZED IT WAS ABOUT TO HIT THE OTHER VEHICLE. NO ALERTS OR WARNINGS

narrative.headline: 2019 Ford Edge: transmission and engine top owner complaints
narrative.summary: The dominant pattern in owner complaints for the Ford Edge of this generation centers on powertrain behavior, with the most common reports describing delayed or unresponsive gear engagement and sluggish acceleration when pressing the accelerator. A secondary cluster of complaints involves the engine and electrical system, where owners describe sudden and complete vehicle shutdown — including loss of hazard lights — attributed to a ground wire separating from the ECM while the vehicle was in motion or stopped at a light. A smaller share of owners also report the vehicle running roughly and stalling without warning at moderate speeds.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle failing to shift into gear promptly and not accelerating immediately when the accelerator pedal is depressed while driving at various speeds · 4 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report a ground wire disconnecting from the ECM causing complete loss of vehicle functionality and an unexpected shutdown while driving, including loss of hazard warning lights · 1 crash

issues.title: Electrical System

issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle running rough and then losing motive power and stalling without warning while driving at moderate speeds

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: The smallest of the top five: 31 reports (4%), with 2 crashes. If you rely on driver-assist features like park assist, test them carefully and stay ready to take over.
